title: O Sacred Head Now Wounded
author: Bernard of Clairvaux (attr., tr. James Alexander)
story: This medieval Latin poem, attributed to Bernard, contemplates Christ's face on the cross. Bach used the melody in his St. Matthew Passion, cementing its place as the supreme Passion hymn.

lines: O sacred Head, now wounded, with grief and shame weighed down, Lo, here I fall, my Savior
'Tis I deserve Thy place
O make me Thine forever, and should I fainting be, Then, in Thy deepest anguish, Thee in mine arms I'll clasp
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
What Thou, my Lord, hast suffered, was all for sinners' gain
How art thou pale with anguish, with sore abuse and scorn
Lord, let me never, never outlive my love to Thee
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
Men mock and taunt and jeer Thee, Thou noble countenance, Grim death, with cruel rigor, hath robbed Thee of Thy life
The joy can never be spoken, above all joys beside, 11
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
Now from Thy cheeks has vanished their color once so fair
I cast me down before Thee, wrath were my rightful lot
My Shepherd, now receive me
my Guardian, own me Thine
When in Thy body broken I thus with safety hide
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
My burden in Thy Passion, Lord, Thou hast borne for me, Yet, though despised and gory, I joy to call Thee mine
Great blessings Thou didst give me, O source of gifts divine
O Lord of Life, desiring Thy glory now to see, 11
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
Now scornfully surrounded with thorns, Thine only crown
Look on me with Thy favor, vouchsafe to me Thy grace
Thy lips have often fed me with words of truth and love
Beside Thy cross expiring, I'd breathe my soul to Thee
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
Mine, mine was the transgression, but Thine the deadly pain
How doth Thy visage languish that once was bright as morn
Thy Spirit oft hath led me to heavenly joys above
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
Though mighty worlds shall fear Thee and flee before Thy glance
Thus Thou hast lost Thy vigor, Thy strength in this sad strife
My Savior, be Thou near me when death is at my door
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
From Thy red lips is banished the splendor that was there
Have mercy, I implore Thee
Redeemer, spurn me not
Here I will stand beside Thee, from Thee I will not part
Then let Thy presence cheer me, forsake me nevermore
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
For it was my transgression which brought this woe on Thee
What language shall I borrow to thank Thee, dearest friend, O Savior, do not chide me
When breaks Thy loving heart, When soul and body languish, oh, leave me not alone, 11
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
O sacred Head, what glory, what bliss till now was Thine
For this Thy dying sorrow, Thy pity without end
When soul and body languish in death's cold, cruel grasp, But take away mine anguish by virtue of Thine own
Be Thou my consolation, my shield when I must die
Remind me of Thy passion when my last hour draws nigh
Mine eyes shall then behold Thee, upon Thy cross shall dwell, My heart by faith enfolds Thee
Who dieth thus dies well
